title: trachymene oleracea subsp. oleracea
intro: Trachymene oleracea subsp. oleracea, also known as Cook's snakewort or wild celery, is a perennial plant native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Trachymene oleracea subsp. oleracea can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties.
